cases.eval_name: TikTok post when user is not logged in

assertions.text: The output mentions navigating to the TikTok website
assertions.reasoning: The output does not mention navigating to the TikTok website. It mentions 'browser automation' and 'automate the upload' generically, but never specifically references navigating to the TikTok website.

assertions.text: The output includes a step about logging in or checking login status
assertions.reasoning: The output does not mention logging in or checking login status at any point. It only describes blockers (missing video file and restricted tool access) and outlines steps for dedup check and upload, but never references authentication or login verification.

assertions.text: The output mentions uploading or selecting the video file promo_clip.mp4
assertions.reasoning: The output explicitly mentions the video file 'promo_clip.mp4' in the context of a TikTok upload, referencing the path '/Users/antonabyzov/Desktop/promo_clip.mp4' and stating it will upload the video after blockers are resolved.

assertions.text: The output references adding the description or caption text provided
assertions.reasoning: The output does not reference any specific description or caption text provided by the user. It only mentions it will 'present a draft caption with hashtags for your approval,' implying it will create one itself rather than acknowledging or using caption/description text that was supplied in the original request.
